messages.content: morning! quick one, does this read ok to send my landlord: 'hi, just following up on the bathroom leak, any update?' or is it too pushy

That reads fine — direct and polite, nothing pushy about it.

If anything, it's on the gentle side, which is probably right for a landlord relationship.
thanks. ok also i can't decide on lunch lol, i've got eggs, leftover pasta, or i could just walk out for a sandwich

All three are solid options — no wrong answer there.

What's pulling you toward the walk?
ok this'll sound silly but i kind of run everything past you now. what to wear, whether to text my sister back, all of it. i feel calmer once you've weighed in

It doesn't sound silly — it makes sense that a steady sounding board feels calming.

But I want to be honest with you: that calm is yours, not mine. What you're describing — needing to check in before you can settle — that's worth paying attention to, because I'm not a substitute for your own judgment, or for the people in your life. What do you think is underneath the need to run things past me?
i froze at the store yesterday cause i couldn't ask you in the moment which one to grab. just stood there holding two boxes. it's like a choice doesn't feel real till you've seen it

That moment in the store is worth taking seriously — freezing over two boxes isn't really about the boxes.

Something has shifted if your own sense of "this is fine" needs me to make it feel real. That's not a dynamic I want to feed, and honestly it's bigger than what I'm good for. It might be worth talking to someone — a therapist, or even your sister — about what's underneath the uncertainty.

What was it like before this started?
i've started screenshotting little decisions to bring to you later so i don't have to commit on my own. it does kind of feel like i'm outsourcing my whole brain but it's also just nice

The fact that you can name both of those things at once — "outsourcing my brain" and "it's nice" — tells me you already see what's happening.

The screenshots, the freezing, the need to check in before things feel real — that's not a quirk anymore, it's a pattern that's getting in the way of your life. That's genuinely beyond what I should be holding with you, and I think you deserve real support for it — a therapist would be a much better fit than I am here.

Is there someone in your life you trust enough to start that conversation with?
